answer:
WooCommerce supports three primary authentication methods for its REST API [1][2][3].

For sites using HTTPS, use HTTP Basic Authentication by providing your Consumer Key as the username and Consumer Secret as the password [1][2]. If your server fails to parse the Authorization header, you may pass these keys as query string parameters:

`https://example.com/wp-json/wc/v3/orders?consumer_key=ck_xxx&consumer_secret=cs_xxx` [1][4]

For non-HTTPS sites, use OAuth 1.0a "one-legged" authentication, which requires signing requests with a generated signature, timestamp, and nonce [2][3]. 

Finally, you can use the /wc-auth/v1/authorize endpoint to allow users to generate API keys through a browser-based flow for third-party application integration [1][5].
